description: ScoreForge OMR test corpus: 100+ real-world scores covering every notation complexity category.
target_accuracy: 99% pixel match on top-100 priority scores

complexity_categories.multi_voice: 2+ simultaneous voices per staff, requiring <backup> element and explicit <voice> tags
complexity_categories.tuplets_complex: Triplets, quintuplets, nested tuplets, cross-beat tuplets
complexity_categories.grace_ornaments: Appoggiatura, acciaccatura, mordents, turns, trills, ornamental runs
complexity_categories.ossia_cue: Ossia staves (alternative notation), cue notes (smaller noteheads from other parts)
complexity_categories.transposing: Instruments written at transposed pitch (Clarinet Bb, Horn in F, etc.)
complexity_categories.percussion: Unpitched percussion notation, drum set, stickings, rolls
complexity_categories.guitar_tab: Classical guitar notation, tablature, fingering indications
complexity_categories.figured_bass: Baroque continuo numerals below bass staff (2, 4, 6, 7, 9, etc.)
complexity_categories.cross_staff_beam: Beams connecting notes on different staves (common in Chopin, Brahms)
complexity_categories.polyrhythm: Simultaneous conflicting rhythmic subdivisions (3 vs 2, 4 vs 3)
complexity_categories.key_time_changes: Mid-piece key signature and/or time signature changes
complexity_categories.repeats_navigation: Repeat barlines, codas, segnos, D.C. al Fine, 1st/2nd volta brackets
complexity_categories.dynamics_articulations: Rich dynamic markings (ppp to fff), hairpins, staccato, accent, tenuto, etc.
complexity_categories.lyrics: Sung text syllabically aligned under notation (single and multi-verse)
complexity_categories.chord_symbols: Jazz/pop chord symbol notation (Cmaj7, G7b9, etc.) above staff
complexity_categories.ensemble_large: Full scores with 4+ simultaneous instrumental parts
complexity_categories.handwritten: Manuscript facsimiles, autographs, poor-quality scans — hardest real-world input

scores.notes: Syllabic lyrics, simple homophonic texture. Baseline vocal score test.
Piano reduction with moderate chords. Tests grand staff with sparse texture.
12/8 compound meter. Vocal line with piano accompaniment. Compound subdivision test.
Single melodic line, clean notation. Pure pitch accuracy test.
Simple vocalise exercise. Single staff baseline.
Two independent voices, imitative counterpoint. Voice separation critical.

scores.work: WTC Book I Prelude No. 1, BWV 846
scores.notes: All arpeggiated chords, no melodic line. High note density (~200 notes/page). Staff-1/staff-2 assignment.
Famous appoggiatura grace note in bar 1. Key change from A min to F maj. D.C. structure.

scores.work: Prelude Op. 28 No. 4 in E minor
scores.notes: Simple melody vs chromatic inner voices. pp hairpin to ppp. Dynamics extraction test.
Classic Alberti bass. Trill ornament bar 6. 2 pages = cross-page continuity.

scores.work: Prelude Op. 28 No. 20 in C minor
scores.notes: Gold standard: 13 bars, entirely 4-voice chords, ppp. Zero tolerance for errors.

scores.work: Nocturne Op. 9 No. 2 in Eb major
scores.notes: Hardest in corpus. Triplet-in-triplet ornamental runs. 4 pages = max context pressure.
Chromatic voice-leading, cross-beat syncopation via ties spanning barlines.
Three independent voices on two staves. Core test for voice/backup element handling.
Grace note ornamental figures, parallel major/minor key change, 1st/2nd volta endings.

scores.work: Christ lag in Todes Banden, BWV 277
scores.notes: SATB on 2 staves. Soprano+Alto on treble (stems up/down). German lyrics. Inner chromatic voices.
4-part SATB with figured bass symbols. Tests figured bass numeral extraction.
~50 pages. Use only pp. 1-3 for testing. Cl in Bb, Hn in Eb transposing. 8+ parts.
Double-stops in single-line instrument. Trill/mordent ornaments. Slur vs tie disambiguation.
Ossia staves appear in orchestral reductions and some piano editions. Source from IMSLP Brahms or Liszt piano transcriptions. Manual download required.
Chord symbol notation (Cmaj7, G7, Am, etc.) is specific to jazz/pop lead sheets. Source from public domain Real Book facsimiles or MuseScore community. Manual acquisition required.

IMSLP autograph manuscript. Tests handwritten notation recognition. Requires IMSLP account or anonymous delay to download. URL: https://imslp.org/wiki/Piano_Sonata_No.11_in_A_major,_K.331/385i_(Mozart,_Wolfgang_Amadeus)
Compare against clean CP-M01. Same piece, different source quality. IMSLP autograph. Manual download required.
Bartók's Mikrokosmos has pieces in 5/8, 7/8, and other irregular meters. Copyright status complex (1926-1939) — check IMSLP carefully. Best source for true aksak/additive rhythm tests.
Brahms frequently writes 3-against-2 cross-rhythm. This intermezzo also has cross-staff beaming. High value for both polyrhythm and cross-staff beam tests. Mutopia URL not found — source from IMSLP.
